source: Cross-implementation JCS vectors supplied by reviewer (Python rfc8785 0.1.4 reference).
purpose: Feed each 'input' to seilx canonicalize. Output bytes and SHA-256 must match exactly. A mismatch means one of the two implementations is wrong and cross-system hashes will not agree.

vectors.name: integers_and_floats_no_exponent
vectors.note: SEILX v0.1 forbids exponent notation on input AND output. Vectors that exercise 1e+30 / 1e-07 exist only in must_reject.

must_reject.name: unsafe_integer_above_2p53
must_reject.reason: Values outside the interoperable integer range -(2^53-1)..(2^53-1) may lose precision when represented as IEEE 754 numbers, and libraries may apply different input-domain restrictions. To guarantee cross-implementation determinism, the SEILX profile requires unsafe integers to be rejected or encoded as strings. This is a SEILX interoperability constraint, not an RFC 8785 requirement.

must_reject.name: duplicate_property_name
must_reject.reason: JSON.parse silently keeps only one of the values. SEILX rejects the input so canonicalization cannot depend on the parser's choice.

must_reject.name: duplicate_property_name_nested_object
must_reject.reason: Regression: duplicate keys inside a nested object must also be rejected at text-scan time — the outer object hides nothing.

must_reject.name: duplicate_property_name_inside_array
must_reject.reason: Regression: duplicate keys inside an object nested in an array must also be rejected.

must_reject.name: lone_high_surrogate
must_reject.reason: A high surrogate without its matching low surrogate cannot be encoded in UTF-8 and would hash differently between implementations.

must_reject.name: nan_literal
must_reject.reason: NaN is not a JSON value and has no canonical representation.

must_reject.name: infinity_literal
must_reject.reason: Infinity is not a JSON value and has no canonical representation.

must_reject.name: exponent_notation_large
must_reject.reason: SEILX v0.1 forbids exponent notation on input. RFC 8785 permits it, but SEILX narrows the input profile so canonical output never contains 'e'/'E'. Represent such values as strings.

must_reject.name: exponent_notation_small
must_reject.reason: Same rule: SEILX v0.1 forbids exponent notation on numeric literals.
